data.title: WORLD CUP | Tuchel says Declan Rice has no injury ahead of England vs Mexico last-16
data.description: England midfielder Declan Rice has no injury and is expected to be fit for Sunday’s World Cup last-16 match against Mexico at the Azteca, coach Thomas Tuchel said. Rice went off late in England’s 2-1 win over the Democratic Republic of Congo after managing nerve pain in his back during the tournament, and Tuchel said the issue was pain rather than an injury, according to Yahoo Sports.

data.title: Refiner Profit Margins Surge as Hormuz Shipping Snarls Linger
data.description:
US crude refiners are posting some of their best profit margins in years, signaling that supply-chain disruptions tied to the Strait of Hormuz are still affecting global markets, according to Bloomberg.

The gains come even as energy shipments through the strait have started to pick up, suggesting lingering logistical kinks continue to support refining profitability.

data.title: Securitize Begins NYSE Trading Under SECZ and Lists Common Stock Onchain
data.description:
Securitize began trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker SECZ on Thursday and placed its newly listed common stock onchain the same day. According to NS3.AI, eligible U.S. investors can access tokenized SECZ through Securitize’s regulated platform after completing onboarding, KYC, AML, and jurisdiction checks.

The SECZ listing followed a merger with Cantor Equity Partners, which brought roughly $400 million in proceeds. Securitize said tokenized SECZ will launch on Avalanche and Solana.

data.title: Polymarket Bettor With $570,000 Weekly Profit Holds $360,000 Position on Portugal vs. Croatia Goals
data.description:
A Polymarket participant who made $570,000 in profit over the past week holds a $360,000 position predicting more than 2.5 total goals in the “World Cup Round of 16: Portugal vs. Croatia” market.

According to Odaily, monitoring by a PPP prediction-market tool identified the address associated with the position as 0x10a6fadcbacd66330862206f6199b197e3ad4d8b.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Carney to Keep North BC Tanker Ban as Alberta to Unveil Pipeline Proposal
data.description:
Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said he will keep the ban on oil tankers off northern British Columbia as Alberta prepares to unveil a pipeline proposal, according to Bloomberg.

Carney also announced billions of dollars in federal funding for energy, mining and transportation projects in British Columbia.

data.title: Saylor Pits Strategy Against Mag 7 as MSTR Options Open Interest Hits 71.9% of Market Cap
data.description:
Michael Saylor compared Strategy (MSTR) with the Magnificent 7 on July 2, calling it Wall Street’s “MoST inteResting” stock as shares rebound from last week’s lows. According to BeInCrypto, a chart Saylor shared shows MSTR options open interest at 71.9% of market cap, versus Tesla (TSLA) at 15.8% and Meta (META) at 10.8%.

Strategy’s latest filing shows 847,363 Bitcoin (BTC) bought for $64.1 billion at an average $75,646 per coin; with BTC near $61,760, the stake is about $54 billion. MSTR rose 12.5% Monday after a capital management overhaul, then fell 6.2% Tuesday to $86.93 as TD Cowen cut its target to $260 from $400; Citi kept Buy but cut its target to $136. Rosen Law Firm opened a securities probe into Strategy.

data.title: Germany Should Pause Coal Plant Closures, Grid Operator Amprion Says
data.description: Germany should halt further closures of coal-fired power plants for now to safeguard electricity supplies as renewable generation expands, according to Bloomberg, citing grid operator Amprion GmbH.

data.title: Iranian President Says Supporting Armed Forces Is His Duty, Iranian Media Reports
data.description:
Iranian media outlet Fars News reported that Iran’s president said supporting the armed forces was his duty and that he would support them with all his strength.

According to Jin10, the report attributed the remarks to Iran’s president.

data.title: Iranian President Rejects Criticism Over Alleged 20 Million-Barrel Oil Offer, Iranian Media Says
data.description:
Iranian media Fars News reported that Iran’s president rejected criticism over “providing 20 million barrels of oil to a certain company,” saying the matter was an “internal information disclosure issue.”

According to Jin10, the president also said that if a similar situation happens again, he would be willing to provide “100 million barrels of oil.”

data.title: Prison Phone Company Aventiv Clears Hurdle for Lenders’ Takeover
data.description:
Aventiv Technologies reached an out-of-court deal that would allow its lenders to take control of the prison phone company from private equity firm Platinum Equity, according to Bloomberg.

The agreement caps a lengthy effort to secure regulatory approval, people familiar with the matter said.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Google Says GTIG Estimated NetNut Botnet at 2 Million Devices Worldwide
data.description:
Google said GTIG estimated the NetNut botnet involved 2 million devices worldwide.

According to Jin10, the statement was attributed to Google.

data.title: STOCKS | European Stocks Rise as Investors Rotate Away From Technology
data.description: European shares advanced as investors rotated away from technology stocks and looked for alternatives in sectors that had been neglected in recent months, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Microsoft Plans to Merge Consumer and Enterprise Copilot Chatbots in August
data.description: Microsoft plans to merge its consumer and enterprise Copilot chatbots in August. According to Jin10, the report was attributed to The Information.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Microsoft Copilot Executive Cuts Low-Value Copilot Features, Report Says
data.description:
A report said a Microsoft Copilot executive is cutting low-value Copilot features.

According to Jin10, The Information reported that Copilot executive Jacob Andrew is reducing low-value Copilot functions.

data.title: Standard Chartered Launches Direct USDC Minting and Redemption for Institutional Clients in Dubai
data.description:
Standard Chartered has launched direct USDC minting and redemption for institutional clients in Dubai, allowing eligible clients to convert U.S. dollars into USDC within their existing banking relationship.

According to NS3.AI, the move connects users to USDC, a stablecoin with a $73.2 billion market capitalization.

The update follows a similar step by BNY, which enabled clients to mint, redeem, and hold USDC three days earlier.

Standard Chartered said any expansion into additional markets will depend on regulatory approvals and market readiness.

data.title: WORLD CUP | Four Barcelona players start for Spain vs Austria
data.description: Spain coach Luis de la Fuente named four Barcelona players in his starting lineup for Spain’s Round of 32 match against Austria in the USA: Lamine Yamal, Pedri, Dani Olmo and Pau Cubarsi. Yamal was included after recovering from a hamstring injury, while Eric Garcia and Joan Garcia were on the bench; Gavi and Ferran Torres were also among the substitutes, according to Yahoo Sports.

data.title: Google Disables Accounts Used by NetNut for Malware Command and Control
data.description:
Google said it has disabled Google accounts that were used by NetNut for malware command and control.

According to Jin10, the company described the accounts as being used for malicious activity related to malware operations.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Google Says It Has Taken Action Against NetNut Residential Proxy Network
data.description:
Google said it has taken action against the “NetNut residential proxy network,” also known as POPA.

According to Jin10, the statement referred to Google’s actions involving the NetNut residential proxy network.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Fuel Shortages Stall Venezuela Rescue Efforts as Cranes Sit Idle
data.description:
Fuel shortages in Venezuela have forced excavators and cranes to stop operating at some rescue sites, hampering recovery efforts after last week’s earthquake, according to Bloomberg.

The lack of fuel has left crews unable to clear debris in parts of La Guaira state, one of the hardest-hit areas.

data.title: Soybeans Drift as China Buying Awaited After US Tariff Statement
data.description: Soybean futures traded between small gains and losses as traders weighed a statement by China’s government on rolling back tariffs on US agricultural products, a move that could pave the way for purchases by the Asian country, according to Bloomberg.
